Module org.flossware.commons
Package org.flossware.commons.util

Class StringUtil

java.lang.Object
org.flossware.commons.util.StringUtil

Same vein as java.util.Objects

1.0

      public static final String STRING_CANNOT_BE_BLANK

  • Method Details

    • requireNonBlank

      public static String requireNonBlank(String string, String message)
      Validates that a string is not null, empty, or whitespace-only.
      Parameters:
      string - the string to validate
      message - the error message to use if validation fails
      Returns:
      the validated string
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the string is blank
      Since:
      1.0

    • requireNonBlank

      public static String requireNonBlank(String string)
      Validates that a string is not null, empty, or whitespace-only using default error message.
      Parameters:
      string - the string to validate
      Returns:
      the validated string
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the string is blank
      Since:
      1.0

    • asUrlEncoded

      public static String asUrlEncoded(String str)
      URL encodes a string using UTF-8 encoding.
      Parameters:
      str - the string to URL encode
      Returns:
      the URL-encoded string
      Since:
      1.0

    • ensureString

      @Deprecated(since="1.22", forRemoval=true) public static String ensureString(String str, String errorMsg)
      Deprecated, for removal: This API element is subject to removal in a future version.
      Use requireNonBlank(String, String) instead
      Checks str to ensure it is not null nor empty. Delegates to requireNonBlank for validation.
      Parameters:
      str - The string to inspect to ensure its not null nor empty.
      errorMsg - The error message within the raised exception if str is null or empty.
      Returns:
      str if it is not null or empty.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if str is blank.

    • ensureString

      @Deprecated(since="1.22", forRemoval=true) public static String ensureString(String str)
      Deprecated, for removal: This API element is subject to removal in a future version.
      Use requireNonBlank(String) instead
      Checks str to ensure it is not null nor empty. Delegates to requireNonBlank for validation.
      Parameters:
      str - The string to inspect to ensure its not null nor empty.
      Returns:
      str if it is not null or empty.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if str is blank.

    • concatWithSeparator

      public static StringBuilder concatWithSeparator(StringBuilder stringBuilder, boolean isSeparatorAtEnd, String separator, Object... objs)
      Concat objects together in stringBuilder using the toString of the objs.
      Parameters:
      stringBuilder - the string builder form whom we will concatenate.
      isSeparatorAtEnd - if true denotes we will always have the separator appended.
      separator - the separator to use between concatenation.
      objs - the objects to concatenate.
      Returns:
      the string builder with concatendated data.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if separator is null
      Since:
      1.0

    • concatWithSeparator

      public static String concatWithSeparator(boolean isSeparatorAtEnd, String separator, Object... objs)
      Concat objects together and return the toString of the concatenation.
      Parameters:
      isSeparatorAtEnd - if true denotes we will always have the separator appended.
      separator - the separator to use between concatenation.
      objs - the objects to concatenate.
      Returns:
      the string representation of the concatenation.
      Since:
      1.0

    • concatWithSeparator

      public static StringBuilder concatWithSeparator(StringBuilder stringBuilder, String separator, Object... objs)
      Concat objects together in stringBuilder using the toString of the objs.
      Parameters:

      separator - the separator to use between concatenation.
      objs - the objects to concatenate.
      Returns:
      the string representation of the concatenation.
      Since:
      1.0

    • isContained

      public static boolean isContained(String str, String contains)
      Return true if str contains contains.
      Parameters:
      str - is the string to examine for containing contains.
      contains - is the string to see if str is contained.
      Returns:
      true if str contains contains.
      Since:
      1.0

    • toCompressedString

      @Deprecated(since="1.22", forRemoval=true) public static String toCompressedString(Serializable serializable)
      Deprecated, for removal: This API element is subject to removal in a future version.
      Use JSON libraries (Jackson, Gson) for safer serialization. This method relies on Java native serialization which has known security vulnerabilities. Scheduled for removal in version 2.0.
      Serializes and compresses a Serializable object to a Base64-encoded string.

      SECURITY WARNING: Java serialization is inherently insecure when deserializing untrusted data. This method should ONLY be used for trusted, internal data. For external data or cross-system communication, use JSON (Jackson, Gson) or Protocol Buffers instead.

      DEPRECATED: This method will be removed in a future version. Migrate to JSON-based serialization for better security and interoperability.

      Parameters:
      serializable - the object to serialize and compress
      Returns:
      the compressed, Base64-encoded string representation
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if serializable is null
      RuntimeException - if serialization fails

    • toString

      @Deprecated(since="1.22", forRemoval=true) public static String toString(Serializable serializable)
      Deprecated, for removal: This API element is subject to removal in a future version.
      Use JSON libraries (Jackson, Gson) for safer serialization. This method relies on Java native serialization which has known security vulnerabilities. Scheduled for removal in version 2.0.
      Serializes a Serializable object to a Base64-encoded string.

      SECURITY WARNING: Java serialization is inherently insecure when deserializing untrusted data. This method should ONLY be used for trusted, internal data. For external data or cross-system communication, use JSON (Jackson, Gson) or Protocol Buffers instead.

      DEPRECATED: This method will be removed in a future version. Migrate to JSON-based serialization for better security and interoperability.

      Parameters:
      serializable - the object to serialize
      Returns:
      the Base64-encoded string representation
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if serializable is null
      RuntimeException - if serialization fails

    • fromCompressedString

      @Deprecated(since="1.22", forRemoval=true) public static <T extends Serializable> T fromCompressedString(String str)
      Deprecated, for removal: This API element is subject to removal in a future version.
      Use JSON libraries (Jackson, Gson) for safer deserialization. This method has critical security vulnerabilities. Scheduled for removal in version 2.0.
      Deserializes and decompresses an object from a Base64-encoded compressed string.

      CRITICAL SECURITY WARNING: Java deserialization of untrusted data is a critical security vulnerability that can lead to Remote Code Execution (RCE). NEVER deserialize data from untrusted sources, user input, or external systems.

      DEPRECATED: This method will be removed in a future version. Use JSON libraries (Jackson, Gson) for safer deserialization with proper validation.

      SECURITY REQUIREMENTS: If you must use this method, you MUST:

      • Authentication: Verify the source is authenticated and authorized
      • Integrity: Verify data integrity using HMAC-SHA256 or digital signatures
      • Encryption: Data should be encrypted in transit and at rest
      • Trusted Sources Only: Only deserialize data you serialized yourself
      • Never from: User input, network requests, external files, databases shared with untrusted systems
      • Logging: Log all deserialization attempts with source information for security auditing

      BUILT-IN PROTECTIONS: This method implements:

      • Strict class whitelist (specific safe classes only, not entire packages)
      • Gadget chain detection (blocks known exploit classes like PriorityQueue, HashMap)
      • Deep inspection (validates nested objects recursively)
      • Depth limiting (prevents DoS via deeply nested structures)
      • Deny-by-default policy (only explicitly whitelisted classes allowed)

      WARNING: These protections reduce but do not eliminate risk. New gadget chains are discovered regularly. The only safe approach is to migrate to JSON-based serialization (Jackson with default typing disabled).

      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the object to deserialize
      Parameters:
      str - the Base64-encoded compressed string to deserialize
      Returns:
      the deserialized object
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if str is blank
      RuntimeException - if deserialization fails or encounters untrusted class

    • fromString

      @Deprecated(since="1.22", forRemoval=true) public static <T extends Serializable> T fromString(String str)
      Deprecated, for removal: This API element is subject to removal in a future version.
      Use JSON libraries (Jackson, Gson) for safer deserialization. This method has critical security vulnerabilities. Scheduled for removal in version 2.0.
      Deserializes an object from a Base64-encoded string.

      CRITICAL SECURITY WARNING: Java deserialization of untrusted data is a critical security vulnerability that can lead to Remote Code Execution (RCE). NEVER deserialize data from untrusted sources, user input, or external systems.

      DEPRECATED: This method will be removed in a future version. Use JSON libraries (Jackson, Gson) for safer deserialization with proper validation.

      SECURITY REQUIREMENTS: If you must use this method, you MUST:

      • Authentication: Verify the source is authenticated and authorized
      • Integrity: Verify data integrity using HMAC-SHA256 or digital signatures
      • Encryption: Data should be encrypted in transit and at rest
      • Trusted Sources Only: Only deserialize data you serialized yourself
      • Never from: User input, network requests, external files, databases shared with untrusted systems
      • Logging: Log all deserialization attempts with source information for security auditing

      BUILT-IN PROTECTIONS: This method implements:

      • Strict class whitelist (specific safe classes only, not entire packages)
      • Gadget chain detection (blocks known exploit classes like PriorityQueue, HashMap)
      • Deep inspection (validates nested objects recursively)
      • Depth limiting (prevents DoS via deeply nested structures)
      • Deny-by-default policy (only explicitly whitelisted classes allowed)

      WARNING: These protections reduce but do not eliminate risk. New gadget chains are discovered regularly. The only safe approach is to migrate to JSON-based serialization (Jackson with default typing disabled).

      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the object to deserialize
      Parameters:
      str - the Base64-encoded string to deserialize
      Returns:
      the deserialized object
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if str is blank
      RuntimeException - if deserialization fails or encounters untrusted class

    • generateUniqueString

      public static String generateUniqueString(String prefix, String suffix)
      Generates a unique string with the string's prefix being prefix and suffix being suffix. Uses UUID to ensure uniqueness even under high-concurrency scenarios.
      Parameters:
      prefix - the prefix.
      suffix - the suffix.
      Returns:
      a unique string containing prefix as prefix and suffix as suffix.
      Since:
      1.0

    • generateUniqueString

      public static String generateUniqueString(String prefix)
      Generate a unique string with prefix as the prefix.
      Parameters:
      prefix - the prefix.
      Returns:
      a unique string containing prefix as prefix.
      Since:
      1.0

    • generateUniqueString

      public static String generateUniqueString()
      Generate a unique string.
      Returns:
      a unique string.
      Since:
      1.0
